description: Created iSTART (Interactive Strategy Training for Active Reading and Thinking) and co-developed Coh-Metrix — the most widely used computational tool for analyzing textual cohesion, readability, and discourse structure. Co-led Writing Pal — the first ITS for writing strategy instruction. Most-cited ASU learning engineering researcher; moved to USC circa 2018.
affiliation: University of Southern California (formerly Arizona State University)
role: Reading and writing ITS researcher; Coh-Metrix and iSTART developer

name: Janet L. Kolodner
description: Created the case-based reasoning (CBR) paradigm and developed the Learning by Design (LBD) framework — project-based learning grounded in design challenges. Co-edited the Learning Engineering Toolkit with Jim Goodell. Intellectual parent of the LE process methodology: iterative, evidence-based, user-centered design of learning systems.
role: Case-based reasoning pioneer; Learning by Design framework developer; co-author of LE Toolkit

name: Daniel L. Schwartz
description: Developed the 'time for telling' principle and 'inventing to prepare for future learning' — showing that generative struggle creates representations that support deeper subsequent learning. Established the productive failure literature demonstrating that effortful incorrect attempts precede better understanding than direct instruction.
role: Learning scientist; inventing-before-learning and productive failure paradigm developer

name: Paul A. Kirschner
description: Co-authored 'Why Minimal Guidance During Instruction Does Not Work' (2006) — the most-cited critique of discovery and problem-based learning (6,800+ citations). Extended Cognitive Load Theory to collaborative learning. Co-authored How Learning Happens with Carl Hendrick and the 4C/ID model with van Merriënboer.
role: CLT applied to collaboration; co-author of 'Why Minimal Guidance Fails'

name: Dragan Gašević
description: Co-founded the Learning Analytics and Knowledge (LAK) conference and the Society for Learning Analytics Research (SoLAR). Established the principle that 'learning analytics should not promote one-size-fits-all' — foundational for equity-aware LE system design. Applied process mining to collaborative learning data, revealing strategy patterns invisible to aggregate statistics.
role: Learning analytics field-builder; LAK conference co-founder; process mining in CSCL

description: Nine-week intensive for educators and designers to apply learning science and data-informed methods to real educational products and contexts; part of OLI professional learning.

name: MIT Learning Engineering and Practice (LEAP) Group
description: Research group applying systems and design methods to STEM and workforce learning; emphasizes AI, simulation, XR, haptics, MOOCs, and curriculum at scale.

name: LearnLab & LearnLab professional programs (CMU / network)
description: Research network and professional offerings (e.g., data analysis and modeling workshops, summer school) connecting cognitive science, LearnSphere/DataShop, and course improvement for formal and informal educators.

name: ASU Learning Engineering Institute & Graduate Certificate
description: Graduate certificate and research network fusing human systems engineering, design, and evidence to improve educational systems; connects students with Learning Engineering Research Network partners.

name: Indiana University Luddy — ISE & human-centered computing
description: Large informatics and engineering school where Intelligent Systems Engineering, human-centered computing, and data-driven design converge—common feeder disciplines for learning engineering careers even where the phrase is not a standalone degree title.

name: Penn Center for Learning Analytics (UPenn GSE)
description: GSE hub for learning analytics and AI in education research, including work that explicitly intersects learning engineering, educational data mining, and design of data-informed learning systems. Related graduate offerings include the online M.S. in Learning Analytics and Artificial Intelligence.

name: Stanford GSE — M.S. Learning Design and Technology (LDT)
description: Residential master’s focused on designing and evaluating technology-enhanced learning environments using learning sciences, design methods, and empirical study—one of the clearest Stanford pathways aligned with learning engineering practice (even when the degree title says LDT, not LE).

name: University of Washington — Learning sciences & design (CoE, HCDE, iSchool)
description: Graduate hub in Learning Sciences & Human Development plus related units (e.g., Learning, Epistemology, and Design Lab in HCDE; learning sciences at the Information School) for research on learning, design, and technology in formal and informal settings—UW’s closest cluster to learning engineering even without a single LE degree name.

name: Purdue School of Engineering Education (ENE)
description: First-in-the-nation school of engineering education; graduate offerings include the online M.S. in Engineering Education, Ph.D. in engineering education research, and the stackable Teaching and Learning in Engineering graduate certificate—explicit “learning engineering” language appears in certificate and course titles.

name: JHU MEd in Learning Design and Technology
description: Master's degree in Learning Design and Technology at JHU School of Education. Houses the Learning Engineering for Next-Generation Systems (LENS) concentration.

name: IEEE 1484.1-2003: Learning Technology Systems Architecture (LTSA)
description: Defines the high-level system components and communication protocols required for interoperable learning technology platforms. Foundational systems architecture standard for the field.

name: IEEE 9274 Family (xAPI / Experience API)
description: Data instrumentation standard governing how learning experiences are tracked, stored in Learning Record Stores (LRS), and analyzed across distributed platforms. Includes JSON-LD application profiles, cybersecurity protocols, and vocabulary registries.

name: IEEE P2247.1 & P2247.2: Adaptive Instructional Systems (AIS)
description: Classifies components, parameters, and interoperability protocols for adaptive instructional systems — engineering blueprints for building systems that dynamically adjust instruction based on real-time learner state.

name: IEEE 1484.20.2: Recommended Practice for Defining Competencies
description: Rigorous data schema for defining human competencies. Supports Integrated Learner Records (ILR) spanning K-12, higher education, workforce, and military environments.

name: IEEE 1484.12.1-2002 & P2881: Learning Object Metadata (LOM)
description: Essential standards for cataloging, discovery, and semantic structuring of digital learning resources across repositories. P2881 is the modernized successor incorporating linked data and accessibility requirements.

name: Open Context Exchange (OCX)
description: OER schema: specification and metadata ontology for publishing Open Educational Resources on the web. Unifies content to a standard vocabulary for discoverability and automated remixing.

name: IEEE 1484.12.1-2002 & P2881: Learning Object Metadata
description: Essential standards for cataloging, discovery, and semantic structuring of digital learning resources across repositories. Enables automated discoverability and cross-platform content reuse.

name: IEEE 1484.1-2003: Learning Technology Systems Architecture (LTSA)
description: Defines the high-level system components and communication protocols required for interoperable learning technology platforms. Foundational systems architecture standard for the field. Sponsoring body: IEEE LTSC.

name: IEEE 9274 Family (xAPI / Experience API)
description: Data instrumentation standard governing how learning experiences are tracked, stored in Learning Record Stores (LRS), and analyzed across distributed platforms. Includes JSON-LD application profiles, cybersecurity protocols, and vocabulary registries. The data infrastructure standard underpinning modern LE telemetry pipelines.

name: IEEE P2247.1 & P2247.2: Adaptive Instructional Systems (AIS)
description: Classifies components, parameters, and interoperability protocols for adaptive instructional systems. Engineering blueprints for building systems that dynamically adjust instruction based on real-time learner state. The most directly LE-relevant IEEE standard family.

name: IEEE 1484.20.2: Recommended Practice for Defining Competencies
description: Rigorous data schema for defining human competencies. Supports Integrated Learner Records (ILR) spanning K-12, higher education, workforce, and military environments. Critical for competency-based education and credential portability.

name: IEEE 1484.12.1-2002 & P2881: Learning Object Metadata
description: Essential standards for cataloging, discovery, and semantic structuring of digital learning resources across repositories. Enables automated discoverability and cross-platform content reuse. P2881 is the modernized successor incorporating linked data and accessibility requirements.

name: Open Context Exchange (OCX)
description: OER schema: specification and metadata ontology for publishing Open Educational Resources on the web. Unifies content to a standard vocabulary for discoverability and automated remixing. Enables interoperable OER ecosystems.

name: IMS Global CASE (Competency and Academic Standards Exchange)
description: Technical standard for expressing and exchanging competency frameworks, learning outcomes, and academic standards in machine-readable form. Enables interoperable knowledge representation across learning platforms — the infrastructure layer for competency-based knowledge models.

name: MIL-HDBK-502A: Acquisition of Training
description: DoD handbook specifying acquisition processes for training systems, including task analysis, learning objective specification, instructional media selection, and evaluation criteria. Directly applicable to large-scale training system procurement and LE deployment in defense contexts.

name: MIL-HDBK-46855A: Human Engineering Program Process and Procedures
description: Adjacent-field resource carried forward from landscape curation; enrichment pending.

name: 1EdTech Comprehensive Learner Record (CLR) Standard
description: Adjacent-field resource carried forward from landscape curation; enrichment pending.

name: Open Badges Specification (1EdTech)
description: Adjacent-field resource carried forward from landscape curation; enrichment pending.

name: Five Whys Root-Cause Analysis Template (ICICLE)
description: Google Doc template for applying the Five Whys technique to identify root causes of learning performance gaps. Designed for the LE needs-analysis phase.

name: Fishbone / Cause-Effect Analysis Template (ICICLE)
description: Google Doc template for Ishikawa (fishbone) cause-and-effect diagrams in learning contexts. Used during LE problem framing and needs analysis to map contributing factors to learning gaps.

name: Performance Task Analysis Template (ICICLE)
description: Google Doc template for structured task decomposition in learning engineering. Guides practitioners through breaking complex performance into observable subtasks to inform instructional design.

name: Learning Engineering Evidence Decision Tracker (ICICLE)
description: Google Drive folder with templates for tracking design decisions alongside the evidence that motivated them throughout the LE process. Supports reproducible, evidence-based practice.

name: SchoolSims — LE-Based Teacher Preparation Simulator (2025)
description: Simulation platform for teacher preparation applying learning engineering principles, cognitive task analysis, and naturalistic decision-making. Uses scenario-based assessment and data-driven iteration.

name: Caliper Analytics
description: Caliper is xAPI's closest sibling — a learning-analytics interop standard from 1EdTech. If you're picking an LMS analytics stack, you'll encounter both.

name: GIFT — Generalized Intelligent Framework for Tutoring
xAPI — Experience API specification
description: The de facto standard for learning-event instrumentation. Every LE platform conversation eventually comes back to xAPI. Runs through the IEEE LTSC, where much of ICICLE's standards work also happens.

name: Yet Analytics — Learning Engineering Tools
description: Commercial learning-engineering platform spanning xAPI data design, instrumentation, and evaluation. One of the few vendors building tools explicitly for the LE workflow rather than generic ed-tech analytics.

name: ASSISTments
description: Free intelligent tutoring platform serving 500,000+ students and 20,000+ teachers, primarily for mathematics. Large research base; built-in E-TRIALS functionality enabling randomized controlled experiments at scale in live classrooms. Pioneered teacher-authored hint sequences and demonstrated that formative assessment integrated with intelligent hints produces significant learning gains.

name: DataShop / LearnSphere
description: World's largest open repository of educational interaction data. Stores student performance logs indexed by knowledge component models from hundreds of studies. The canonical infrastructure for empirical validation of knowledge representations and EDM research. If a KC model predicts student learning curves, it can be validated here.

name: xAPI / Tin Can API
description: Standard for tracking and communicating learning activity data across platforms, devices, and contexts. Foundation for interoperable Learning Record Stores (LRS). Key infrastructure standard enabling federated learning telemetry pipelines for LE analytics.

name: OpenSimon Toolkit (CMU Simon Initiative)
description: Flagship open infrastructure for LE research globally. Comprises DataShop (data repository), LearnSphere (analytics infrastructure), CTAT (cognitive tutor authoring tools), OLI (courseware), DiscourseDB (NLP for learning), LightSide (automated text coding), and Bazaar (intelligent collaborative chatbot). Enables researchers to build, test, and study LE systems without proprietary infrastructure.

name: CTAT — Cognitive Tutor Authoring Tools
description: Authoring environment for building model-tracing and example-tracing cognitive tutors. Authors encode domain knowledge as production rules (model-tracing) or annotated examples (example-tracing). The primary open-source tool for operationalizing expert knowledge into runnable knowledge component models.

name: H5P — HTML5 Interactive Content
description: Open-source framework for creating interactive learning content (quizzes, interactive video, branching scenarios, simulations) that runs in any browser and LMS. Widely used in LE for rapid prototyping of formative assessment and interactive instruction. Integration with Moodle, Canvas, Brightspace, and WordPress makes it accessible to practitioners at all technical levels.
